article_meta.title: Kayla Bartkowski / GettyMarjorie Taylor Greene Came So Close to Getting the JokeWhat the Georgia representative learned

reasoning: The model correctly identified that the 'article' is actually a homepage/table of contents from The Atlantic rather than a single article, and produced a neutral, descriptive summary. It did not adopt any loaded framing because there was no single article to inherit framing from. The summary is factual and descriptive throughout.

custom_reasoning.spin: No loaded verbs used; all language is neutral and descriptive.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All claims are factual descriptions of what appears on the page.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: No subjective judgments presented as fact.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Language is measured and clinical throughout.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about anyone's thoughts or motivations.
custom_reasoning.slant: No slant detectable; the summary is a neutral description of a webpage.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Given the source is a homepage rather than an article, there are no perspectives to omit.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Adjectives used are neutral and factual (e.g., 'diverse coverage').
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No deference to any particular source type.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: The summary is entirely neutral — appropriate given the source is a homepage.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Not applicable in the traditional sense since this is a homepage, not a reported article.
